label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which sentence is NOT true based on the given information about Bobby Moresco. First, let's go through each option one by one. Starting with option (A): "His father did not support his work as a bartender." Wait, the passage mentions that his father told him to "Stop this craziness and get a job; you have a wife and daughter." But the jobs he did in Hollywood were driving a cab and working as a waiter. The text doesn't specifically mention him being a bartender. So maybe the option is incorrect because it's referring to a job that's not mentioned. Also, the father's disapproval was about his acting career, not necessarily the specific job as a waiter or bartender. So if the option says his father didn't support him being a bartender, but in reality, he was a waiter, then (A) is not correct. So (A) might be the false statement. Option (B): "Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs." The passage describes his struggles in the 70s making only $2k, moving to Hollywood, working odd jobs, then after his brother's death he moved back and started writing. Then he had a break with the play and later writing Crash. So yes, his life had ups and downs before the success in 2005. So (B) is true. Option (C): "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The text says that after his brother was murdered in 1983, he moved back and started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen. The play Half-Deserted Streets was based on his brother's killing. So (C) is true. Option (D): "Moresco grew up in New York's Hell's Kitchen which is a few blocks east of Broadway." The passage states that Hell's Kitchen is next to Broadway and he had to make a long walk a few blocks east. So the neighborhood is next to Broadway, which would mean it's west of Broadway if he had to walk east to get to it. Wait, if he had to walk a few blocks east from Hell's Kitchen to get to Broadway, that would mean Hell's Kitchen is west of Broadway. But the option says Hell's Kitchen is a few blocks east of Broadway. That would be the opposite. So (D) is incorrect. Wait, the original text says "Hell's Kitchen lies right next door to Broadway, and the bright lights attracted Bobby from the time he was a teen. [...] the long walk a few blocks east."
